channel.title: Libraries and software on gabri.xyz
channel.description: Recent content in Libraries and software on gabri.xyz

item.title: Squiggles
item.description: Squiggles.jl is a Julia package to accelerate 1D cross correlations of seismic signals using GPUs! We use KernelAbstractions.jl to run on any GPU supported by the JuliaGPU ecosystem.

item.title: HighSeas
item.description:
HighSeas is a Julia package focused on accelerating Sequences of Earthquakes and Aseismic Slips simulations using the Spectral Boundary Integral Method on GPUs.

Associated manuscript: Benedetti, G., Heimisson, E. R. (2026, submitted). HighSeas.jl Accelerating rate-and-state simulations with GPUs

How to cite

Benedetti, G., & Heimisson, E. R. (2026). HighSeas.jl v0.7.0 - Main release (v0.7.0). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19372420

@software{benedetti_2026_19372420,
  author       = {Benedetti, Gabriele and
                  Heimisson, Elías Rafn},
  title        = {HighSeas.jl v0.7.0 - Main release},
  month        = apr,
  year         = 2026,
  publisher    = {Zenodo},
  version      = {v0.7.0},
  doi          = {10.5281/zenodo.19372420},
  url          = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19372420},
}

item.title: Fractalizer
item.description:
Fractalize the edges of any given curve or closed ring using either a template or random noise!

Check it out on github! https://github.com/gbene/Fractalizer.jl

item.title: SirGridsAlot
item.description:
Rapidly create and plot in 2D/3D regular Euclidean tilings with Python.

Check it out on github! https://github.com/gbene/SirGridsAlot

item.title: Fracability
item.description: Fracture networks are essential for the analysis and modelling of mechanical and hydraulic properties of rock masses. Recently, the use of Digital Outcrop Models (DOMs) provided a solid framework for the collection of large and quantitative datasets from which different properties can be extracted. Because of the complex nature of exposed rock outcrops, statistical model fitting can sometimes be challenging. Areas covered by rock debree, vegetation patches or simply the outer boundaries of the outcrop can introduce right-censoring bias and can often lead to parameter underestimation. Tools are needed to correct for estimating the correct distribution parameters while taking into account this bias. Survival analysis techniques, although usually applied in function of time and not length, accomplishes exactly this.

item.title: Pzero
item.description:
PZero is a Python open-source 3D geological modelling application, leveraging various libraries, with a user-friendly graphical user interface allowing to perform most standard geomodelling data management and analysis tasks, explicit surface interpolation, and advanced implicit interpolation.

Check it out on github! https://github.com/gecos-lab/PZero
